heading: Regulatory appeals process, ombudsman, and alternative dispute resolution
label: 4806 Regulatory appeals process, ombudsman, and alternative dispute resolution
notes: Termination of Administrative Conference of United StatesFor termination of Administrative Conference of United States, see provision of title IV of , set out as a note preceding , Government Organization and Employees.Pub. L. 104–52section 591 of Title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , each appropriate Federal banking agency and the National Credit Union Administration Board shall establish an independent intra-agency appellate process. The process shall be available to review material supervisory determinations made at insured depository institutions or at insured credit unions that the agency supervises.September 23, 1994
any appeal of a material supervisory determination by an insured depository institution or insured credit union is heard and decided expeditiously; and
appropriate safeguards exist for protecting the appellant from retaliation by agency examiners.

children.heading: Comment period
children.label: c Comment period
children.text: Not later than 90 days after , each appropriate Federal banking agency and the National Credit Union Administration Board shall provide public notice and opportunity for comment on proposed guidelines for the establishment of an appellate process under this section.September 23, 1994

children.heading: Establishment required
children.label: 1 Establishment required
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , each Federal banking agency and the National Credit Union Administration Board shall appoint an ombudsman.September 23, 1994

children.label: A
children.text: act as a liaison between the agency and any affected person with respect to any problem such party may have in dealing with the agency resulting from the regulatory activities of the agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: assure that safeguards exist to encourage complainants to come forward and preserve confidentiality.

children.heading: Alternative dispute resolution pilot program
children.label: e Alternative dispute resolution pilot program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 18 months after , each Federal banking agency and the National Credit Union Administration Board shall develop and implement a pilot program for using alternative means of dispute resolution of issues in controversy (hereafter in this section referred to as the “alternative dispute resolution program”) that is consistent with the requirements of subchapter IV of chapter 5 of title 5 if the parties to the dispute, including the agency, agree to such proceeding.September 23, 1994

children.label: A
children.text: be fair to all interested parties to a dispute;

children.label: B
children.text: resolve disputes expeditiously; and

children.label: C
children.text: be less costly than traditional means of dispute resolution, including litigation.

children.label: A
children.text: an evaluation of that pilot program;

children.label: B
children.text: the extent to which the pilot programs meet the standards established under paragraph (2);

children.label: C
children.text: the extent to which parties to disputes were offered alternative means of dispute resolution and the frequency with which the parties, including the agencies, accepted or declined to use such means; and

children.label: D
children.text: any recommendations of the Conference to improve the alternative dispute resolution procedures of the Federal banking agencies and the National Credit Union Administration Board.

children.heading: Implementation of program
children.label: 4 Implementation of program
children.text: At any time after completion of the evaluation under paragraph (3)(A), any Federal banking agency and the National Credit Union Administration Board may implement an alternative dispute resolution program throughout the agency, taking into account the results of that evaluation.

children.heading: Coordination with existing agency ADR programs
children.label: 5 Coordination with existing agency ADR programs

children.heading: Evaluation required
children.label: A Evaluation required
children.text: If any Federal banking agency or the National Credit Union Administration maintains an alternative dispute resolution program as of , under any other provision of law, the Administrative Conference of the United States shall include such program in the evaluation conducted under paragraph (3)(A).September 23, 1994

children.heading: Multiple ADR programs
children.label: B Multiple ADR programs
children.text: No provision of this section shall be construed as precluding any Federal banking agency or the National Credit Union Administration Board from establishing more than 1 alternative means of dispute resolution.

children.label: i
children.text: examination ratings;

children.label: ii
children.text: the adequacy of loan loss reserve provisions; and

children.label: iii
children.text: loan classifications on loans that are significant to an institution; and

children.label: B
children.text: does not include a determination by a Federal banking agency or the National Credit Union Administration Board to appoint a conservator or receiver for an insured depository institution or a liquidating agent for an insured credit union, as the case may be, or a decision to take action pursuant to section 1831 of this title or , as appropriate.osection 1790a of this title

children.heading: Independent appellate process
children.label: 2 Independent appellate process
children.text: The term “independent appellate process” means a review by an agency official who does not directly or indirectly report to the agency official who made the material supervisory determination under review.

children.heading: Alternative means of dispute resolution
children.label: 3 Alternative means of dispute resolution
children.text: The term “alternative means of dispute resolution” has the meaning given to such term in .section 571 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: any final agency decision involving any claim against an insured depository institution or insured credit union for which the agency has been appointed conservator or receiver or for which a liquidating agent has been appointed, as the case may be;

children.label: B
children.text: any final action taken by an agency in the agency’s capacity as conservator or receiver for an insured depository institution or by the liquidating agent appointed for an insured credit union; and

children.label: C
children.text: any other issue for which the appropriate Federal banking agency or the National Credit Union Administration Board determines that alternative means of dispute resolution would be appropriate.

children.heading: Effect on other authority
children.label: g Effect on other authority
children.text: Nothing in this section shall affect the authority of an appropriate Federal banking agency or the National Credit Union Administration Board to take enforcement or supervisory action.
